game_review: This game ended up being far more immersive than I expected. The world feels alive, side quests are meaningful, and exploration constantly rewards curiosity. Performance issues still exist in some larger areas, but the core gameplay loop is strong enough that I kept coming back for more.

What surprised me most was how naturally the world design encouraged exploration. Small villages, hidden caves, faction conflicts, and random encounters constantly pulled me away from the main story in the best possible way.

Crafting and progression systems work well.
